name: fig4-preemption
description: Licensing posteriors under preemption: rare (sparse opportunities) vs dense preemption. Expected values are the plotted coordinates of Figure 4 (label fig:posterior-means) in Reynolds, 'Grammaticality de-idealized'.
source: papers/retarget/grammaticality-de-idealized/section3.tex, pgfplots coordinates (lines 678-695), verified against main.aux (figure 4, p. 31) on 2026-07-09; quantiles cross-checked against the Beta(1,b) closed form x_p = 1 - (1-p)^(1/b).
tolerance_note: Expected values are rounded to 4 decimal places in the paper's plot coordinates, so agreement within 5e-5 is exact agreement.

scenarios.description: Sparse opportunities: per-step effective preemption mass 0.01, no positive tokens. Posterior stays near the prior; CrI spans nearly the whole unit interval.
Dense preemption: per-step effective preemption mass 5, no positive tokens. Posterior both falls and concentrates.
